nodes.name: Load DLQ Row
nodes.notes: Service-key read (bypasses RLS). fullResponse keeps a single wrapper item even when the row is missing ([] body would otherwise split into zero items and starve Guard). Non-throwing.

nodes.name: Claim Row (open->replayed)
nodes.notes: Atomic compare-and-swap: filter status=eq.open so two concurrent Replay clicks can't both dispatch. return=representation -> [] means we lost the race.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', correlation_id: $('Guard').item.json.correlation_id || null, message: 'a replay is already in progress' }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'queued', correlation_id: $('Guard').item.json.correlation_id || null, message: 'replay dispatched to ' + $('Guard').item.json.workflow_name }) }}

nodes.name: Dispatch To Source
nodes.notes: Re-run the source synchronously so we know success/fail. continueErrorOutput splits: out0 -> resolve, out1 -> bump toward the cap. Errors are CAUGHT here so 05 never fails -> its own errorWorkflow (none) can't create a mis-attributed 05-dlq-replay row.
